advice.Diet: Focus on a plant-based, Mediterranean-style diet. Prioritize soluble fiber (oats, beans, lentils) which binds to cholesterol, and low-Glycemic Index (GI) foods to stabilize blood sugar. Completely avoid trans fats and heavily processed sugars. Opt for healthy unsaturated fats like avocados and olive oil.
advice.Lifestyle: Implement 150 minutes of moderate cardiovascular aerobic exercise weekly. Focus on weight management as even a 5% body weight reduction significantly improves metabolic markers. Prioritize 7-8 hours of sleep to regulate cortisol and insulin sensitivity.

Metabolic Syndrome.significance: Cluster of conditions increasing heart disease/diabetes risk.

advice.Diet: Strict carbohydrate management is essential. Fill half your plate with non-starchy vegetables (spinach, broccoli), 1/4 with lean proteins (fish, chicken, tofu), and 1/4 with whole grains. Eliminate all sugar-sweetened beverages entirely space out meals to stabilize glucose.
advice.Lifestyle: Engage in light physical activity (like a 15-minute walk) immediately after meals to help muscles absorb glucose from the bloodstream, mimicking insulin. Regular at-home blood sugar monitoring is highly advised.

Diabetes Type 2 Risk.significance: High blood sugar levels indicative of insulin resistance.

advice.Diet: Immediate fast-acting sugar (fruit juice). Balanced meals.
advice.Lifestyle: Monitor blood sugar. Carry emergency snacks.

advice.Diet: Dramatically increase Heme Iron (red meat, salmon, poultry) and Non-Heme Iron (lentils, spinach, pumpkin seeds). CRITICAL: Always pair iron-rich foods with Vitamin C (citrus fruits, bell peppers, tomatoes) to multiply absorption. AVOID drinking coffee, tea, or milk within 2 hours of meals as they severely block iron absorption.
advice.Lifestyle: Pace yourself to manage chronic fatigue. Consult a doctor for clinical iron supplements, ensuring you take them on an empty stomach with a glass of orange juice.

Microcytic Anemia (Iron Deficiency).significance: Small red blood cells often due to low iron.

advice.Diet: Vitamin B12 sources: Meat, eggs, dairy. Folate: Leafy greens.

Macrocytic Anemia (Vit B12/Folate).significance: Large red blood cells often due to Vit B12/Folate deficiency.

advice.Diet: Papaya leaf extract. Vitamin B12/Folate rich foods.
advice.Lifestyle: Avoid injury/contact sports. Soft toothbrush. Monitor bleeding.

Thrombocytopenia.significance: Low platelet count; risk of bleeding. (Dengue risk).

Leukocytosis (Infection Risk).significance: High WBC often indicates bacterial infection.

Leukopenia (Viral Risk).significance: Low WBC often seen in viral infections.

advice.Diet: Aggressively reduce saturated fats (found in red meat and full-fat dairy) and eliminate artificially created trans fats. Increase your intake of soluble fiber (found heavily in oatmeal, kidney beans, and apples) to actively block cholesterol absorption in the digestive tract. Incorporate Omega-3 fatty acids from walnuts, flaxseeds, and fatty fish.
advice.Lifestyle: Incorporate rigorous cardiovascular exercise to elevate 'good' HDL cholesterol while lowering 'bad' LDL. Stop smoking, as smoking lowers HDL and damages arterial walls.
